english: Chicken wing stuffed with minced meat

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product entry; no specific stuffed/deboned variant exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, cooked — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for a cooked chicken-based dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for stuffed deboned chicken wing. A00GJ (cooked chicken meat) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for stuffed chicken wing as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical stuffed chicken wing weighs approximately 80 g after cooking and represents a single-piece serving as commonly served in Singapore hawker centres and restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Stuffed Chicken Wing — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 with review_status reviewed. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for Stuffed Chicken Wing (F11020049). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for cooked chicken wing with stuffing (SR Legacy #05066) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for stuffed chicken wing. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous mixed meat-and-noodle dish with low carbohydrate content (5.5 g/100g). The small carbohydrate fraction (glass noodles, GI ~26–32 per University of Sydney GI Database) is diluted by high protein (17.9 g) and fat (11.9 g), which slow gastric emptying. Glycemic load per 80 g serving = (35 × 5.5 × 0.80) / 100 = 1.5 g, confirming negligible glycemic impact. Estimation basis documented per Veritas source policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 437 mg per 100 g — approaching the upper threshold for a single dish component
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one wing (≈80 g, ~350 mg sodium) and request baked rather than deep-fried preparation; avoid additional soy-based dipping sauces to keep total meal sodium below 600 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.5 g/100g) and high protein (17.9 g/100g) supporting glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Stuffed chicken wing is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions; the low carbohydrate content and high protein slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose rise, but glass noodle stuffing should be minimised and sweet dipping sauces avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.2 g/100g) and sodium (437 mg/100g) combined with typical deep-frying preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, choose baked preparation to reduce saturated and trans fat exposure, limit to one wing per meal, and pair with high-potassium vegetables to partially offset the sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end products (AGEs) and trace trans fat that amplify joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should request baked preparation to eliminate AGE formation; if baked, the dish is more RA-neutral and the mushroom stuffing variant provides some anti-inflammatory beta-glucan benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stuffed chicken wing (酿鸡翅) is a popular Singaporean and Chinese-heritage dish in which the wing bones are carefully removed and the skin is filled with a savoury mixture of minced pork or chicken, glass noodles, mushrooms, and aromatics seasoned with soy sauce, oyster sauce, and spices. The stuffed wing is then typically deep-fried until golden and crispy, though baked versions are increasingly available. The result is a compact, flavour-dense piece with a crispy exterior, juicy meat interior, and a subtly chewy noodle-and-meat filling. Per 100 g, the dish provides 201 kcal, 17.9 g protein, 11.9 g fat, and only 5.5 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate option. Sodium is elevated at 437 mg/100g due to soy and oyster sauce in the stuffing, which is the primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic impact is low (estimated GI ~35) owing to the dominant protein and fat matrix. The dish is commonly served at Singapore hawker centres, zi char stalls, and Chinese restaurants as a starter or side dish, and is popular during festive occasions. It is halal-certifiable when pork is substituted with chicken or beef mince. Clinically, it is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management in moderate portions but should be consumed cautiously by individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to sodium and saturated fat content.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database (F11020049) at confidence 0.95 with reviewed provenance. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy closest analogue and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database principles, clearly documented. All mandatory multilingual names for a Singaporean dish are present.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #05066 (chicken wing, cooked) as HPB Foodo does not separately report saturated fat for this item; this is an adapted value and should be replaced with a direct HPB measurement if available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy for this specific stuffed preparation. 3. Trans fat (0.1 g/100g) is a trace estimate consistent with deep-frying in vegetable oil; set as a positive numeric value rather than -1 as it is clinically relevant for RA and CVD guidance. 4. Potassium (210 mg/100g) is an estimate from USDA SR Legacy chicken wing data; not directly reported by HPB for this item. 5. cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo for this item and no sufficiently close approved-source analogue was available to justify a numeric estimate. 6. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for stuffed chicken wing exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16. 7. Halal flag set to true reflecting the input dietary_tags; this applies when pork mince is substituted with halal-certified chicken or beef mince — traditional recipes may use pork. 8. Aliases consistency self-check (q8): all values in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array — english: ['Stuffed Chicken Wing', 'Stuffed chicken wing', 'Deboned stuffed chicken wing', 'Chicken wing stuffed with minced meat'] ✓; chinese: ['酿鸡翅', '釀雞翅'] ✓; malay: ['Sayap Ayam Berinti'] ✓; tamil: ['அடைத்த கோழி இறக்கை'] ✓; hindi: ['भरवां चिकन विंग'] ✓; romanized: ['Niang Ji Chi'] ✓. Previous iteration failure was a mismatch between hindi alias 'भरवां चिकन विंग' in aliases_by_language and 'भरवां चिकन विंग' in flat aliases — corrected to exact match in this iteration.
